title: There are some mistakes with the README.markdown and Selection Sampling code improvement
author: efemazlumoglu
body:
### Brief Intro

https://github.com/raywenderlich/swift-algorithm-club/pull/997
I have created this PR for that issue, there are some mistakes in the README files and also a little improvement for the Selection Sampling example function.

### More Details

swap(&a[i], &a[r]) can be written like a.swapAt(i, r)
That would be remove the error called "Overlapping accesses to 'a', but modification requires exclusive access; consider calling MutableCollection.swapAt(_:_:)"

title: LinkedList`s removeAll method and default deinit  may cause stack overflow
author: NickMeepo
body:
When triggering removeAll or deinit of very long LinkedList ( like 1...10000000 integers storage)，you will get stack overflow. The  reason is nodes` recursive releasing.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10824752/160771370-98fc8b40-28fb-4f26-89a8-9c07a071ed27.png)

From the image above you will see recursive node deinit pushing into the stack accumulatively and this will cause crash with size increasing.

I suggest to fix this issue by rewriting removeAll, not simply 'self.head = nil', but using a while-loop  to releasing each node, and calling removeAll in LinkedList deinit.

title: [Question] Translation to pt-br
author: amadeu01
body:
### Translation to pt-br

We have a huge community in Brazil of iOS developers. But, not all of them are fluent in English, we could make it more accessible to them by translating the content.


Can I make a branch or a section and start the translation of the content?

title: Array2D - Enhance to include ability to create 2D array using either [col][row] or [row][col] subscripting.
author: l-rettberg
body:
### Brief Intro

The Array2D algorithm is based on using column then row, ie [col][row] when accessing cells.  Not sure if that is the commonly used approach?  I thought the most common was row, column, ie [row][col], but perhaps that's really up to the developer.

### More Details

Suggest updating the algorithm to include a boolean flag that the caller can define whether the 2D array should be created as [col][row] or [row][col] for most flexibility.

title: Queue Optimized - dequeu is not O(1)
author: chipbk10
body:
### Brief Intro
Queue Optimized - dequeue is not O(1)

### More Details
```
    if array.count > 50 && percentage > 0.25 {
      array.removeFirst(head)
      head = 0
    }
```
If the condition happens, the complexity will be `O(n/4)` ~ `O(n)`
I think you should use `LinkedList` to implement a `Queue` to archive `O(1)` in all cases of `dequeue`

title: Efficiency of LinkedList.count
author: MattGeimer
body:
https://github.com/raywenderlich/swift-algorithm-club/blob/5d551049a02cccd00b046937750fd6d736065f76/Linked%20List/LinkedList.swift#L38-L50

Isn't this solution less efficient than having a stored property? The current implementation of `count` seems to be O(n), but can be reduced to O(1) by incrementing or decrementing the value when performing modification operations. For example, the following code will iterate through the while loop in `count` 28 times:

```
var myList = LinkedList(array: Array<Int>(0 ..< 10))

print(myList.count)

myList.append(myList.count)

print(myList.count)
```
